| | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

The design looks military—and Musk called it “the third revolution in warfare.”| | | Don't want to receive our publications? | | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | | | | Experimental military tech. # Opinion:
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/ee04eefb-8294-40ff-9da5-eaaf00768e3f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|

* * *
When the Trump administration announced a change in leadership and tactics for Operation Metro Surge in the Twin Cities after the shooting of Alex Pretti, many on the Right and Left viewed the decision as the Department of Homeland Security bending the knee to the mob.
There were reasons for believing that. Instead of Border Patrol Commander Gregory Bovino leading his men in mass sweeps to arrest illegal aliens in places like Home Depot parking lots, Border Czar Tom Homan is in charge and he has emphasized ICE and Border Patrol agents will be now focusing on "targeted" arrests.
Homan has said the ultimate goal is to have Operation Metro Surge end in the near future, but that will depend on local radicals ending their threats and acts of violence towards federal agents. So far, he has sent 700 out of 3,000 agents home.
It is understandable why these latest developments have led to the Left rejoicing and the Right seething. The confrontational tactics and rioting by Leftist agitators seemed to have worked to stop mass deportations, the two opposing sides say.

But in the days since the change in tactics, some on the Left have expressed disappointment as federal agents are still operating in Minneapolis and St. Paul at a heightened level. They have expressed their concerns on the far-left platform, Bluesky.
"By the way, for those of you not in MN: Homan's strategy was to withdraw some agents, so it felt like we were in the denouement of the crisis, and then escalate hard against observers. They're being incredibly aggressive with observers now. National media may not want to check out just yet," said Will Stancil, a liberal activist and frequent target of X's AI, Grok.

"I don't even think they got rid [of] the...700\. And Homan is worse than Bovino," one user replied.
"For all the 'drawdown' talk ICE continues to be all over Minneapolis. Feels endless," Stancil also posted.

On February 3 in Minneapolis, video showed ICE agents arresting "observers" for allegedly interfering with their operations. Meaning, agents under Homan still have a directive to not let people get in the way of lawful police actions.

Homan has further touted county jails are cooperating by honoring ICE detainers to prevent illegal aliens with criminal charges from being released back into the public.
On February 7, Minnesota State Patrol assisted in dispersing an unlawful assembly of anti-ICE protesters from the Whipple Federal Building. Multiple arrests were made. MSP and the Hennepin County Sheriff's Office have increased their presence to act as a buffer between hostile crowds and federal agents.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/94833eae-d71e-472a-a895-4b0470657d2a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
So did the Trump administration really back down in Minnesota? It does not appear that way. What really matters is DHS' ability to make arrests to meet the daily numbers required for mass deportations.
Do I believe the mass sweeps by Border Patrol are more effective? I think so because the unpredictability of agents appearing encourages illegal aliens to self-deport, but at the end of the day, the tactics need to maintain public support through elections so President Trump's mandate can continue uninterrupted. More time is needed in Minneapolis to determine whether the administration's shift is effective in maintaining the operational tempo _and_ public support.

clean_body: So did the Trump administration really back down in Minnesota? It does not appear that way... | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? So did the Trump administration really back down in Minnesota? It does not appear that way... | | | Feb 14| | | ∙| | Guest post ---|---|--- | | | | | | | | | | | | Opinion: | | ---|---|--- Help us replace the mainstream media by becoming a PolitiBrawl Member: When the Trump administration announced a change in leadership and tactics for Operation Metro Surge in the Twin Cities after the shooting of Alex Pretti, many on the Right and Left viewed the decision as the Department of Homeland Security bending the knee to the mob. There were reasons for believing that. Instead of Border Patrol Commander Gregory Bovino leading his men in mass sweeps to arrest illegal aliens in places like Home Depot parking lots, Border Czar Tom Homan is in charge and he has emphasized ICE and Border Patrol agents will be now focusing on "targeted" arrests. Homan has said the ultimate goal is to have Operation Metro Surge end in the near future, but that will depend on local radicals ending their threats and acts of violence towards federal agents. So far, he has sent 700 out of 3,000 agents home. It is understandable why these latest developments have led to the Left rejoicing and the Right seething. The confrontational tactics and rioting by Leftist agitators seemed to have worked to stop mass deportations, the two opposing sides say. A messagr from our sponsor (piece continues below) | | ---|---|--- Did you know some credit cards can actually help you get out of debt faster? Yes, it sounds crazy. But it's true. The secret: Find a card with a "0% intro APR" period for balance transfers. Then, transfer your debt balance and pay it down as much as possible during the intro period. No interest means you could pay off the debt faster. Piece continues But in the days since the change in tactics, some on the Left have expressed disappointment as federal agents are still operating in Minneapolis and St. Paul at a heightened level. They have expressed their concerns on the far-left platform, Bluesky. "By the way, for those of you not in MN: Homan's strategy was to withdraw some agents, so it felt like we were in the denouement of the crisis, and then escalate hard against observers. They're being incredibly aggressive with observers now. National media may not want to check out just yet," said Will Stancil, a liberal activist and frequent target of X's AI, Grok. Will Stancil @whstancil.bsky.social By the way, for those of you not in MN: Homan's strategy was to withdraw some agents, so it felt like we were in the denouement of the crisis, and then escalate hard against observers. They're being incredibly aggressive with observers now. National media may not want to check out just yet Sat, 07 Feb 2026 03:38:26 GMT Stancil's post gained over 15,000 likes and many comments voicing agreement. "I don't even think they got rid [of] the...700\. And Homan is worse than Bovino," one user replied. "For all the 'drawdown' talk ICE continues to be all over Minneapolis. Feels endless," Stancil also posted. Will Stancil @whstancil.bsky.social For all the "drawdown" talk ICE continues to be all over Minneapolis. Feels endless Wed, 04 Feb 2026 15:24:00 GMT On February 3 in Minneapolis, video showed ICE agents arresting "observers" for allegedly interfering with their operations. Meaning, agents under Homan still have a directive to not let people get in the way of lawful police actions. Homan has further touted county jails are cooperating by honoring ICE detainers to prevent illegal aliens with criminal charges from being released back into the public. On February 7, Minnesota State Patrol assisted in dispersing an unlawful assembly of anti-ICE protesters from the Whipple Federal Building. Multiple arrests were made. MSP and the Hennepin County Sheriff's Office have increased their presence to act as a buffer between hostile crowds and federal agents. | | ---|---|--- So did the Trump administration really back down in Minnesota? It does not appear that way. What really matters is DHS' ability to make arrests to meet the daily numbers required for mass deportations. Do I believe the mass sweeps by Border Patrol are more effective? I think so because the unpredictability of agents appearing encourages illegal aliens to self-deport, but at the end of the day, the tactics need to maintain public support through elections so President Trump's mandate can continue uninterrupted. More time is needed in Minneapolis to determine whether the administration's shift is effective in maintaining the operational tempo and public support. | A guest post by| Reporter at MostlyPeaceful.media. Essentially, you learn what it’s like to be a prisoner of war. There were 57 men and 3 women in the class. One of the women immediately stood out — a helicopter pilot who was relentlessly upbeat, totally undaunted by the challenge, and genuinely ecstatic to be there... even while we were being deprived of food and routinely getting roughed up. I knew I needed more of that energy in my life. I asked her out, and two years later, we were married. Fast forward 26 years and Laura and I are raising our two boys with the Michigan values of family, hard work, and integrity. There’s no better teammate to have by my side in this campaign for Congress. ♥️🇺🇲 Thank you,

Hi friend, Joseph Rivet here, Kristen’s husband! Happy Valentine’s Day, or as we call it in our house, Kristen’s favorite day of the year. I’m not kidding, friend. On our very first Valentine's Day together, she told me "don't screw it up." That was many moons ago, and I’d like to think I’ve done a pretty good job of keeping up with her expectations. Every year, I still make sure to pull off something special, and now that she’s spending so much time in D.C., this one has to be _even more_ memorable. **That’s where you come in:** I’d love for Kristen to be able to celebrate her favorite day of the year knowing that she met her mid-quarter fundraising goal. So I have to ask: [**Will you chip in $3 to Kristen’s campaign today? Your support will ensure she has the resources she needs to keep this seat, and helps me give the gift of a stress-free Valentine’s Day 😉.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/125390715/601634414/-1606559032?refcode=em-2.14.26-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NjAyNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzZThjMGY5OC1jMjA5LWYxMTEtODMyZS0wMDIyNDgyYTk3MzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=E0Cb3SkFpqUdVL7BcjqNbt73-i2qTqg0qI7sNhP4ve4=) __If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: __ | | | [Chip in $10 Now](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/125390718/601634417/-1606559032?refcode=em-2.14.26-all-a&express_lane=true&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NjAyNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzZThjMGY5OC1jMjA5LWYxMTEtODMyZS0wMDIyNDgyYTk3MzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=E0Cb3SkFpqUdVL7BcjqNbt73-i2qTqg0qI7sNhP4ve4=)

name: Virginians For Fair Elections
subject: This is working — help us keep going
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Virginians for Fair Elections
body:
We still need your help to hit our $10,000 goal by tomorrow.
# [](https://act.virginiansforfairelections.com/go/131?refcode=em20260214-174&t=1&akid=174%2E12592%2EWgL4TH)
We wanted to share a quick progress update.
**We’re making real progress toward our $10,000 mid-month goal, but we’re not there yet.**[**With the deadline approaching fast, can you chip in $25 to help us close the gap before midnight on February 15?**](https://act.virginiansforfairelections.com/go/41?c0=em_ms_vafe_20260214_1.EiYqlE&amount=25&refcode=20260214-174&t=2&akid=174%2E12592%2EWgL4TH)
By tomorrow, Virginians for Fair Elections needs to raise the remaining funds to keep our digital ads on the air. These ads are critical to making sure voters hear the truth about the redistricting amendment before confusion and misinformation take hold.
**Free and fair elections are the cornerstone of our democracy — and right now, that is under real threat.**
Across the country, Trump and MAGA Republicans are pushing unprecedented efforts to redraw congressional maps mid-decade in an attempt to rig the upcoming midterm elections. As states like Florida move forward without voter approval, Virginians risk losing fair representation in Washington if we do nothing.
That’s why this amendment matters.
It gives Virginians a responsible way to act — one that is temporary, voter-approved, and transparent. Voters will be able to see the proposed maps before casting a ballot, and Virginia’s bipartisan redistricting process will resume after the 2030 census.
_Our digital ads are how we explain all of this clearly to voters across Virginia._
Our ads make clear that this isn’t about partisan games. It’s about leveling the playing field, restoring fairness in our elections, and protecting democracy for future generations.
This campaign is powered by everyday Virginians who care deeply about fairness and transparency. Every dollar raised goes toward reaching voters with accurate information at the moment it matters most.
[**If you are able, please chip in****$25 to help us keep our digital ads on the air and keep this YES on fair elections campaign moving forward.**](https://act.virginiansforfairelections.com/go/41?c0=em_ms_vafe_20260214_1.EiYqlE&amount=25&refcode=20260214-174&t=3&akid=174%2E12592%2EWgL4TH)
**[Donate $25 ››](https://act.virginiansforfairelections.com/go/41?c0=em_ms_vafe_20260214_1.EiYqlE&amount=25&refcode=20260214-174&t=22&akid=174%2E12592%2EWgL4TH)**
Thank you for being a part of this fight,
Virginians for Fair Elections

[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_vafe_footer?refcode=em&t=23&refcode2=174_12592_WgL4TH&akid=174%2E12592%2EWgL4TH)
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end.
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://vafe.actionkit.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=24&akid=174%2E12592%2EWgL4TH)
* [Update My Personal Information](https://vafe.actionkit.com/signup/update-contact-information/?&t=25&akid=174%2E12592%2EWgL4TH)

Contributions or gifts to Virginians for Fair Elections are not tax deductible.

subject: No days off
body:

body:
Happy Valentine’s Day!

| Happy Valentine’s Day, friend! name: Team Katie Porter
subject: Our deadline is fast approaching
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Governor 2026
body:
In a campaign that’s shaping up to be a race to get our message in front of undecided voters, that’s very bad news for us.
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21486?t=1&akid=40854%2E3422442%2EwdFELh)
Peter,
ICYMI: Our billionaire opponent poured another $9.3 million into his campaign, bringing his total self-funding up to **over $38 million.**
That’s enough to _majorly_ tip the scales in his favor.
He’s already bought TV ads across the state, with more sure to come.
In a campaign that’s shaping up to be a race to get our message in front of undecided voters, that’s very bad news for us.
**[We set a $10,000 mid-month fundraising goal to make sure we still have the grassroots support needed to compete. Can you chip in to help us hit our goal?](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20260214_1_ak.xrtAkx&refcode=em20260214-40854&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=40854%2E3422442%2EwdFELh)**
**The good news** is that Katie Porter has a winning message that earns people’s support. She’s going to lower costs, fight for Medicare for All, and protect our rights.
Her record bringing down health care costs and going toe-to-toe with Donald Trump in Congress means more than anything a billionaire can buy.
She needs supporters like you to have her back before our deadline at midnight tomorrow. Thousands of people chipping in right now is the show of strength this campaign needs.
**[Peter, rush $5 to our campaign before midnight tomorrow to show that grassroots power beats billionaire spending every time.](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20260214_1_ak.xrtAkx&refcode=em20260214-40854&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=4&akid=40854%2E3422442%2EwdFELh)**
[Donate ›››](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20260214_1_ak.xrtAkx&refcode=em20260214-40854&t=23&akid=40854%2E3422442%2EwdFELh)
Thank you,
Team Katie Porter

Katie is running to be California’s next governor because California and our country need a leader who will stand up to Trump to lower costs, protect our rights, and combat climate change. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4g-em-footer?refcode=em20260214-40854&amounts=&t=24&refcode2=40854_3422442_wdFELh&akid=40854%2E3422442%2EwdFELh)**

---
Peter,

This Black History Month, we honor and recognize the extraordinary achievements of Black Americans across our nation’s history. This month also invites us to reflect on the work still ahead of us to achieve racial justice and true equity in our nation. I grew up during segregation in North Carolina, Peter. I wouldn’t attend a desegregated school until I was 13. Today, I’m immensely proud to be the first Black woman to serve my district in Congress.

The progress we have made in just my lifetime is astounding. _But there is still so much more to be done. _ **While Republicans try to gerrymander Black voices out of Congress, restrict access to the ballot box, and roll back hard-fought civil rights protections, we have a responsibility to push forward. ** The right to vote, the promise of equal representation, and the opportunity to be heard in our democracy are not partisan issues. They are American principles. We cannot take that progress for granted. We have to defend it. We have to expand it. And we have to elect leaders who understand what’s at stake. **[If you believe in this campaign, I hope you’ll consider pitching in today — any amount helps us out as we head into the final stretch before the primary.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/125391562/601637189/-87142129?amount=10&source=VF_EM_FR_021426_B1_BHM&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTc5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiZWE3MjZmMi1jNDA5LWYxMTEtODMyZS0wMDIyNDgyYTk3MzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=t_joKsAPrFpTi9lkl7GfXczhZ63PeaScIo1TkzYPo0I=)** | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process now.

clean_body: If you’d like to unsubscribe, | | = Peter, This Black History Month, we honor and recognize the extraordinary achievements of Black Americans across our nation’s history. This month also invites us to reflect on the work still ahead of us to achieve racial justice and true equity in our nation. I grew up during segregation in North Carolina, Peter. I wouldn’t attend a desegregated school until I was 13. Today, I’m immensely proud to be the first Black woman to serve my district in Congress. The progress we have made in just my lifetime is astounding. But there is still so much more to be done. While Republicans try to gerrymander Black voices out of Congress, restrict access to the ballot box, and roll back hard-fought civil rights protections, we have a responsibility to push forward. The right to vote, the promise of equal representation, and the opportunity to be heard in our democracy are not partisan issues. They are American principles. We cannot take that progress for granted. We have to defend it. We have to expand it. And we have to elect leaders who understand what’s at stake. = | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process now. | = | = | = | = | = | = With gratitude, Valerie Foushee * | | | ---|---|--- = Valerie is fighting to protect access to health care, working families, and to reform our criminal justice system. But she needs your help to continue to do this great work in Congress. Our data shows Republicans are currently in trouble if we don't secure our base immediately. name: Nikki Budzinski
subject: I need to be direct
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Nikki for Congress
body:
|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/125392932/601641689/-1228673924?refcode=em-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjQwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjOTJkY2RjYi1jNjA5LWYxMTEtODMyZS0wMDIyNDgyYTk3MzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=-S-B313DFgf6S5eZhnJvb8QWDFF_giAa1bKgs1RabdU=)
I hate to be so direct, but this moment calls for it:

**[We’re quickly approaching one of our last fundraising deadlines before the primary election in Illinois. Will you chip in $3, then let me explain the stakes ahead?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/125392933/601641690/-1228673924?refcode=em-2.14.26-all-b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjQwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjOTJkY2RjYi1jNjA5LWYxMTEtODMyZS0wMDIyNDgyYTk3MzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=-S-B313DFgf6S5eZhnJvb8QWDFF_giAa1bKgs1RabdU=)**
| **[Donate $3](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/125392934/601641692/-1228673924?refcode=em-2.14.26-all-b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjQwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjOTJkY2RjYi1jNjA5LWYxMTEtODMyZS0wMDIyNDgyYTk3MzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=-S-B313DFgf6S5eZhnJvb8QWDFF_giAa1bKgs1RabdU=)**
---

When I meet with folks throughout the district, it’s clear that their needs aren’t being met by the GOP majority. Working families are struggling to make ends meet -- and national Republicans are doing nothing.

**I couldn’t be more different: This campaign is about protecting working people from the harm that the Trump Administration is causing, tackling the affordability crisis, and doing our part to flip the House majority.

That’s why I just released an Affordability Agenda – focused on tackling the five biggest costs weighing on families' wallets. [To learn more, read the Op-Ed I just published in FOX News.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/125392936/601641694/-254336232?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjQwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjOTJkY2RjYi1jNjA5LWYxMTEtODMyZS0wMDIyNDgyYTk3MzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=-S-B313DFgf6S5eZhnJvb8QWDFF_giAa1bKgs1RabdU=&emci=f2a1b5c5-c609-f111-832e-0022482a9733&emdi=c92dcdcb-c609-f111-832e-0022482a9733&ceid=13891387)**

With early voting underway, Illinoisans are already making their voices heard at the ballot box. We need a surge of grassroots support to fund our outreach -- from knocking on doors, to planting yard signs, and meeting folks where they are at.

And even though speaking out has made me a target of attacks from Trump himself, I’m undeterred. The work is worth it -- and I need your support to protect Central and Southern Illinois.

**[If you’re able, will you consider chipping in $3 -- or any amount you’re comfortable with -- as we head into the next phase of this race?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/125392938/601641695/-1228673924?refcode=em-2.14.26-all-b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjQwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjOTJkY2RjYi1jNjA5LWYxMTEtODMyZS0wMDIyNDgyYTk3MzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=-S-B313DFgf6S5eZhnJvb8QWDFF_giAa1bKgs1RabdU=)**

clean_body: | = I hate to be so direct, but this moment calls for it: = | = When I meet with folks throughout the district, it’s clear that their needs aren’t being met by the GOP majority. Working families are struggling to make ends meet -- and national Republicans are doing nothing. I couldn’t be more different: This campaign is about protecting working people from the harm that the Trump Administration is causing, tackling the affordability crisis, and doing our part to flip the House majority. That’s why I just released an Affordability Agenda – focused on tackling the five biggest costs weighing on families' wallets. With early voting underway, Illinoisans are already making their voices heard at the ballot box. We need a surge of grassroots support to fund our outreach -- from knocking on doors, to planting yard signs, and meeting folks where they are at. And even though speaking out has made me a target of attacks from Trump himself, I’m undeterred. The work is worth it -- and I need your support to protect Central and Southern Illinois. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = | = | | = | = | | = | = Thank you, Nikki | = = = Nikki is a champion for working families – from leading the fight to pass a $15 minimum wage in Illinois, to bringing manufacturing back to America. body:
My new resolution calls on Congress to tax the ultra-rich and reinvest that money into our communities’ needs. | | | [](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.X6XUC7u8ppFLNtMO_55SZlNseb7gEYIOhm_EWnhzEOZKmTtnV3k2h4V2x_O3H7rdW2az5rgktBwzxQ8Ml9Wgkmv85qW-UsBonL4KRa0bcNqdICU6T3NBDdNIZG7w7wEWhE3KUtd-3AvUmQV2eqr7CDF78PmklUFCURpwhwqdlADO-kTUgw78fHQ9MxV53GkMU5kQNU1nNeAxHAYnSLyu7_W-AB4EmEpurN0lSlskLTNHps6S18DnOrSWpW4GI5bxwWV4N9vqu-BiDnKxpVVqfD3eZsjtk8oNFR4GZi1xOUO4fArbuTwLPOKDejbhmbT6eARPTCEKyupNdrPJbtgNM3_FBqbTXgIUM99X-GjesEdbSc6nspIjdclu0x_X5mj_pkseP3WZJafW3gE4Clm0tg/4o5/Xb4fJOsAQfmMnbggf_RhtQ/h0/h001.YrIfxv2It07KqM9QbV0KXZrJ_AZ3iaQbDlMGtEx8xlo)

Friend, Our government has money for war and genocide, and money for agents to kill, abduct, and detain us—but not money to feed or house us, or to cover our healthcare. _All_ of this money is taxpayers’ money. It belongs to us. But we’ve been watching a massive transfer of wealth from the people to billionaires and corporations. This has reached new heights with a corrupt billionaire as president. Trump’s net worth has grown by over $4 billion in the past year. He made the largest cuts to Medicaid and food assistance in history, while using the presidency to enrich himself, his billionaire allies, and the corporate donors who spent millions on his campaign. From the private prison/detention and fossil fuel industries to corporations involved in militarized violence, surveillance, and polluting technology—these donors have seen huge returns on their investments, at our expense. Many of these industries also donate to members of Congress on both sides of the aisle. That’s why people in power keep giving our public taxpayer money to those at the top, rather than our communities’ needs. So **I just introduced the**** _Defund the Oligarchs, Fund Our Communities_****resolution** to demand Congress end the system of subsidies, tax breaks, and federal contracts for billionaire and corporate donors. That system enables fascism and oligarchy, not democracy. **My new resolution calls on Congress to tax the ultra-rich and reinvest that money into our communities’ needs—such as housing, healthcare, and education.** [**Please sign on today to support my new resolution and demand our government use our taxpayer money to serve the people, not billionaires.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm3-inXg7XQKmpx2SINKyl6WQz_vkJ3ZJKcUzkkSwqwfKGeA77vOoOShpp-YonFcsjiP5yD_objAFsWX6e_LysP13wqEM5N9UhIN2VXsYdV22dGhiak8puIcrXNm7NH11i-hs3JmzUUoPzlishqkaXpM2P4D4egW5oEmhRkFyqkomT5JPbnLgeDLJ-OuOJvRJ-M5pzY1GZmCcuJ5n-826Q8DTsLM_3SBt0_nkJiR0IvRdTQ8gZ-5TZ5yamQRmZHlFFubh0QCSk4r_lw4b4Y5vXd8WKKmfWnpVOQfdNHkszGW01lxGKcWwfsCuJhEPGT_nrXaGlBPhwMGKZphx6bX-af29MdnaaiZhKVN1WSIKxEpkKAizYXViZbkfaP0PfqUJJEncE9t_LNAYFCKjLg-j4Eex/4o5/Xb4fJOsAQfmMnbggf_RhtQ/h1/h001.W42J4f-_8698qFOhA4AJmKRDk8rAxqWMGJwMFxAYChY) | [ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm3-inXg7XQKmpx2SINKyl6WQz_vkJ3ZJKcUzkkSwqwfKGeA77vOoOShpp-YonFcsjiP5yD_objAFsWX6e_LysP13wqEM5N9UhIN2VXsYdV22dGhiak8puIcrXNm7NH11i-hs3JmzUUoPzlishqkaXpM2P4D4egW5oEmhRkFyqkomT5JPbnLgeDLJ-OuOJvRJ-M4QeF6adbt2LMBU5PbcF4sYWtApG6qE3hqM6KH-YgexqVju7k03PFx47ds1kSwjvCCVMUvE-9V05STHUFFqVDNtekAHsp60Huyqy5Ul_9iTNF88AiPT51QSnh_At0rtLogUacPFQRKkIxYI1mh0DYbHNkmqlrmG07xusmTQLxScfSFFJrT8hTtOPhjDWj5C7geRxIc4cCboB7mJOvPXEMX_/4o5/Xb4fJOsAQfmMnbggf_RhtQ/h2/h001.VBG5G8u4v1juZDt1tmi8emdZxNvdMLd-UF-acbFDZ2k)

Thank you for taking action and standing with me for justice for all. Please feel free to read more information in the email I sent yesterday, too.
In solidarity,
Rashida
\---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: **Rashida Tlaib******
Date: Fri, Feb 13, 2026
Subject: Sign to help me defund billionaire oligarchs and fund our communities:
To: dpwillis67@gmail.com
Friend,
Families are struggling to afford healthcare, utilities, groceries, and housing. Meanwhile, billionaire oligarchs keep buying political influence, rigging the system in their favor, and getting richer.
Over the past year, Trump’s net worth has grown by over $4 billion. As president, he takes every chance he gets to enrich himself and his friends, often illegally.
While enacting massive tax cuts for the rich and sending unprecedented billions to private contractors that run ICE detention centers, Trump has slashed life-saving funds for the poor and working class.
I’m fighting back in Congress, including by**introducing a new**** _Defund the Oligarchs, Fund Our Communities_****resolution** with other progressive colleagues.
This legislation calls for ending the system of corporate tax breaks and federal contracts for billionaire-backed companies that bankroll political campaigns.
It demands Congress tax the rich and big corporations while reinvesting in our communities’ needs—such as healthcare, housing, education, and other essentials.
[**Please sign on today to call on Congress to support my new resolution to _Defund the Oligarchs and Fund Our Communities_.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm3-inXg7XQKmpx2SINKyl6WQz_vkJ3ZJKcUzkkSwqwfKGeA77vOoOShpp-YonFcsjiP5yD_objAFsWX6e_LysP13wqEM5N9UhIN2VXsYdV22dGhiak8puIcrXNm7NH11i-hs3JmzUUoPzlishqkaXpM2P4D4egW5oEmhRkFyqkomT5JPbnLgeDLJ-OuOJvRJ-M7YNCe8BH1kh46tpBEzOMokSMn-uWHSkbwALxBZv94hOKmfeUJvNOxAc2rpUJ1JjGr-MyYry3UF9vsbHJ4yQpXQeUhEwt5nSoJowCwrN8qhN9mRZ7x8YnFwG6hTVR36nIcg74vwvcCPALeY8vWv82lCWV-WDmdKVY28bHNyOA8pPMKaEW-WBxqQYKsSEqTFP-eo9AuE6MqWaCmcWrUAJJqP/4o5/Xb4fJOsAQfmMnbggf_RhtQ/h3/h001.DrbNXRF_wJEIbPIeMMsKmFEACrInVXrMZZZPjCMnzps)
[ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm3-inXg7XQKmpx2SINKyl6WQz_vkJ3ZJKcUzkkSwqwfKGeA77vOoOShpp-YonFcsjiP5yD_objAFsWX6e_LysP13wqEM5N9UhIN2VXsYdV22dGhiak8puIcrXNm7NH11i-hs3JmzUUoPzlishqkaXpM2P4D4egW5oEmhRkFyqkomT5JPbnLgeDLJ-OuOJvRJ-M5JKHltOGN3ZREEp2wF-b6JhJNu1YibfCrnUqeYtKhVI-HqFa-IsSOQZkVQ0VSa2zqWnknby4UT4AFznb7_XOjqNUuKWub7Cq0vANcW6yhjiCEjlyjHfdbVQAr4T6iGZhIoNkSLNF0jowSud1yzyN7eOJiWDK_J_evQMroKRnvo3OCWex5IPVbAw0EuED9n2roTNRwgurvGQBFpIW3i07J1/4o5/Xb4fJOsAQfmMnbggf_RhtQ/h4/h001.6UtH6SAYtQ6MpH_FUfBDKnDOXN5pS2AuTqCw-tnrThw)

In the last election, Trump’s biggest donors included the fossil fuel, crypto, and private prison/detention industries. They made huge returns on their investments: For every million they spent, they gained around $18 billion on average.
Trump is helping concentrate their wealth even further through tax breaks, corrupt agreements, government contracts, and industry-friendly policies that harm our communities. **Almost all of this is paid for by American taxpayers.**
This money belongs to us. These are public resources, which should be redirected to fund people’s needs.
Instead of being responsive to the needs of the people, many lawmakers on both sides of the aisle do the bidding of their billionaire and corporate donors.
**We can’t meet people’s needs if billionaires and corporations are writing the rules. To ensure a real democracy where our leaders actually represent us and our needs, we must change this system.** **That’s part of how we fight fascism and how we fight for economic, racial, social, and environmental justice.**
**Congress must make the ultra-rich pay their fair share** , break up corporate monopolies, and end the campaign finance system that enables billionaire and corporate control over our government.
**Congress must also reinvest our taxpayer dollars into our communities** , and help build an economy that supports democracy instead of undermining it.

[**If you agree that public money should serve the people rather than billionaires, please sign the petition to support the**** _Defund the Oligarchs, Fund Our Communitie_****s resolution.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm3-inXg7XQKmpx2SINKyl6WQz_vkJ3ZJKcUzkkSwqwfKGeA77vOoOShpp-YonFcsjiP5yD_objAFsWX6e_LysP13wqEM5N9UhIN2VXsYdV22dGhiak8puIcrXNm7NH11i-hs3JmzUUoPzlishqkaXpM2P4D4egW5oEmhRkFyqkomT5JPbnLgeDLJ-OuOJvRJ-M7jxkFw6DrGF0k8qZt0f89jYhbsREwhvEdOJuNzb4aaf1ADFQdJ_3_Cu995DJbjIBY1n0gFDxPJ7Bc4RYRTTxicSg18ISdrW6ynC8sxlY4IRrVMLKcQXcylzrIXnSHtIB-2ydieBNyO9MhmSxUJXwhgYlODVb-y8dbvSM48uhXoEeI3fx9lUFyTkYa7gdZ9nC5xLL15n4OGrx1TLuJfIJVH/4o5/Xb4fJOsAQfmMnbggf_RhtQ/h5/h001.AP57clHSM4o1-EzdaxDOU7E5nOEXqWX5xktnldMFrrw)
Thank you. We won’t be fooled by billionaires’ attempts to dehumanize and divide us. We see how they’re stealing from us, and instead of falsely blaming our neighbors, we’re joining together and demanding major changes.
We must remember that we have power. It’s always outside movements that move institutions like Congress and the Oval Office to make change.
Although we’re up against big money, we have the power of numbers. The United States has 900 billionaires; there are MANY more of us than them. Together, we can take back our government and our democracy _for the people_.
With you in the fight for better,
Rashida

This has reached new heights with a corrupt billionaire as president. Trump’s net worth has grown by over $4 billion in the past year. He made the largest cuts to Medicaid and food assistance in history, while using the presidency to enrich himself, his billionaire allies, and the corporate donors who spent millions on his campaign. From the private prison/detention and fossil fuel industries to corporations involved in militarized violence, surveillance, and polluting technology—these donors have seen huge returns on their investments, at our expense. Many of these industries also donate to members of Congress on both sides of the aisle. That’s why people in power keep giving our public taxpayer money to those at the top, rather than our communities’ needs. So I just introduced the Defund the Oligarchs, Fund Our Communitiesresolution to demand Congress end the system of subsidies, tax breaks, and federal contracts for billionaire and corporate donors. That system enables fascism and oligarchy, not democracy. My new resolution calls on Congress to tax the ultra-rich and reinvest that money into our communities’ needs—such as housing, healthcare, and education. | Thank you for taking action and standing with me for justice for all. Please feel free to read more information in the email I sent yesterday, too. In solidarity, Rashida \---------- Forwarded message --------- From: Rashida Tlaib** Date: Fri, Feb 13, 2026 Subject: Sign to help me defund billionaire oligarchs and fund our communities: To: dpwillis67@gmail.com Friend, Families are struggling to afford healthcare, utilities, groceries, and housing. Meanwhile, billionaire oligarchs keep buying political influence, rigging the system in their favor, and getting richer. Over the past year, Trump’s net worth has grown by over $4 billion. As president, he takes every chance he gets to enrich himself and his friends, often illegally. While enacting massive tax cuts for the rich and sending unprecedented billions to private contractors that run ICE detention centers, Trump has slashed life-saving funds for the poor and working class. I’m fighting back in Congress, including byintroducing a new** Defund the Oligarchs, Fund Our Communitiesresolution with other progressive colleagues. This legislation calls for ending the system of corporate tax breaks and federal contracts for billionaire-backed companies that bankroll political campaigns. It demands Congress tax the rich and big corporations while reinvesting in our communities’ needs—such as healthcare, housing, education, and other essentials. In the last election, Trump’s biggest donors included the fossil fuel, crypto, and private prison/detention industries. They made huge returns on their investments: For every million they spent, they gained around $18 billion on average. Trump is helping concentrate their wealth even further through tax breaks, corrupt agreements, government contracts, and industry-friendly policies that harm our communities. Almost all of this is paid for by American taxpayers. This money belongs to us. These are public resources, which should be redirected to fund people’s needs. Instead of being responsive to the needs of the people, many lawmakers on both sides of the aisle do the bidding of their billionaire and corporate donors. We can’t meet people’s needs if billionaires and corporations are writing the rules. To ensure a real democracy where our leaders actually represent us and our needs, we must change this system. That’s part of how we fight fascism and how we fight for economic, racial, social, and environmental justice. Congress must make the ultra-rich pay their fair share , break up corporate monopolies, and end the campaign finance system that enables billionaire and corporate control over our government. Congress must also reinvest our taxpayer dollars into our communities , and help build an economy that supports democracy instead of undermining it. Thank you. We won’t be fooled by billionaires’ attempts to dehumanize and divide us. We see how they’re stealing from us, and instead of falsely blaming our neighbors, we’re joining together and demanding major changes. We must remember that we have power. It’s always outside movements that move institutions like Congress and the Oval Office to make change. Although we’re up against big money, we have the power of numbers. The United States has 900 billionaires; there are MANY more of us than them. Together, we can take back our government and our democracy for the people. With you in the fight for better, Rashida Rashida Tlaib for Congress PO Box 32777 Detroit, MI 48232 | Paid for by Rashida Tlaib for Congress | | Sent via . To update your email address, change your name or address, or to stop receiving emails from Rashida Tlaib for Congress, please .

name: Squad Victory Fund
subject: These four seats are at the top of the right wing’s target list
body:
[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/18910?refcode=em20260214-1937-foote&t=1001&akid=1937%2E342411%2EVnzdRr ]Squad Victory Fund

Peter,

Right now, too many of our communities are living under constant threat —
whether it’s neighborhoods facing immigration raids and intimidation,
skyrocketing prices, or extremists coming for our rights and fundamental
freedoms. Far too often, those abuses go unchecked.

Alexandria, Ayanna, Ilhan, and Rashida are fighting back — in Congress and
alongside the communities they represent.

They’re pushing for dignity, accountability, and justice when others
choose silence. And because of that, their seats are at the very top of
the right wing’s target list heading into November.

Defending these seats isn’t about political ego or insider games. It’s
about whether our communities continue to have champions who will show up,
speak out, and fight — or whether those voices are replaced by politicians
who rubber-stamp cruelty.

That’s where the Squad Victory Fund comes in.

We exist to help defend our bold, progressive leaders and build lasting
power in the communities they serve. That work takes early organizing,
real resources, and people-powered support.

Tomorrow is our mid-month fundraising deadline, and we need all the
support we can get to stay on track. Every contribution helps us protect
these seats in November and ensure our communities aren’t pushed further
into the shadows.

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20260214-movement.jmDj_s&refcode=em20260214-1937&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1002&akid=1937%2E342411%2EVnzdRr ]If you believe our communities deserve leaders who will fight for them
— not sell them out — please chip in $3 before tomorrow’s
deadline. This is one of those moments where showing up matters the most.

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20260214-movement.jmDj_s&refcode=em20260214-1937&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1021&akid=1937%2E342411%2EVnzdRr ]Contribute ››

In solidarity,

The Squad Victory Fund

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe:

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.squadvictoryfund.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=1937%2E342411%2EVnzdRr

Paid for by The Squad Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, and Rooted in Community Leadership PAC.

In addition to what’s legally required, The Squad Victory Fund does not accept contributions from: (1) corporations, associations, or their PACs; (2) individuals registered as federal lobbyists or under FARA; (3) from the PACs, lobbyists, or SEC-named executives of fossil fuel companies; or (4) from the Fraternal Order of Police.

Contributions or gifts to The Squad Victory Fund are not tax deductible.

Contributions to The Squad Victory Fund will be allocated equally among: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, and Rooted in Community Leadership PAC.

Any contributor may designate their contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if any contributor makes a contribution that, when allocated, would exceed the amount that the contributor may lawfully give to any participant.

After contributing to The Squad Victory Fund, you will automatically be signed up to receive email updates from Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, and Rashida Tlaib for Congress. You may of course opt-out by unsubscribing at any time.

To contribute via check, please address to The Squad Victory Fund c/o MBA Consulting Group, PO Box 15845, Washington D.C., 20003

Email us: info@squadvictoryfund.com

name: Jocelyn Benson
subject: can I get your eyes on this?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JOCELYN BENSON FOR GOVERNOR]
body:
[ stdin# ]Jocelyn Benson for Governor
Hi,

Before I ask you to donate to my campaign for Governor of Michigan, I want
to take a moment to explain why your support matters so much right now.

[ https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/2250?t=1001&akid=1527%2E181023%2EA62vTK ]I hope you’ll keep reading. But if you’re ready to support our campaign
now, you can donate here:

[ https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/2250?t=1002&akid=1527%2E181023%2EA62vTK ]Donate Now →

Michigan is going to be one of the most competitive governor’s races in
the country this year.

That’s not surprising. Races here are often decided by slim margins, and
in a close race, every conversation, every organizer, and every voter
contact makes a real difference. I’m confident we will win this – because
I’ve been here before. This is my fourth statewide campaign, and I won my
last statewide race by 14 points. I know how to win tough races. We win by
showing up everywhere, listening to people, and delivering results that
matter.

That’s what I’ve done as Secretary of State. And it’s exactly how I’ll
lead as governor.

Still, I know this won’t be easy. There are people who doubt what a
people-powered campaign can accomplish, and people who don’t believe
Michigan is ready to keep moving forward with leadership rooted in
fairness, accountability, and service.

That’s why I’m asking for your help.

We have an important mid-month fundraising deadline coming up, and what we
raise before then will shape what we’re able to do next as this campaign
continues to grow.

[ https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/2250?t=1003&akid=1527%2E181023%2EA62vTK ]If you’re able, will you make a donation today to help keep this
campaign strong and moving forward?

[ https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/2250?t=1004&akid=1527%2E181023%2EA62vTK ]Donate Now →

Every contribution helps build a campaign powered by people who care
deeply about Michigan’s future.

People who want a governor who will protect our democracy, strengthen
public education, and make Michigan the best place to be a kid, raise a
kid, and call home.

I hope you’re with me.

Jocelyn

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with supporters like you.
Unsubscribe: https://act.jocelynbenson.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe-jbmi/?t=1005&akid=1527%2E181023%2EA62vTK

name: David Hogg
subject: Eight years ago today
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE
body:
Here’s something I want you to know today.
#
Peter,
**Eight years ago today was the worst day of my life.**
A 19-year-old who never should have had access to a gun walked into my high school with an AR-15 and took the lives of 17 students and educators, injuring 17 others. Nothing prepares you for the sound of your classmates crying for their friends — or for how that moment stays with you forever.
I’ve spent the years since trying to make sure no one else has to live with that kind of pain.
I’ll be honest: this work is soul-crushing. No one chooses to spend their life reliving the worst day they’ve ever experienced. People who survive violence don’t organize because they want to — we do it because it feels mandatory. Because if we don’t fight to stop it from happening again, who will?
For a long time, it felt like we were screaming into the void. “Thoughts and prayers” from leaders who refused to act, while the violence continued.
But here’s something that matters — and something I want you to know today:
**This work _is_ making a difference.**
Despite what you’ll often see in the news, gun violence in America has declined substantially from its peak during the COVID era. After historic spikes in 2020 and 2021, national gun homicide and shooting rates have fallen for several consecutive years and are now among the lowest they’ve been in nearly a decade.
That didn’t happen by accident.
It happened because people like you stayed in this fight — and because we started changing who’s in government. Leaders like Maxwell Frost, the first Gen Z member of Congress, helped push the Biden administration to create the first-ever White House Office of Gun Violence Prevention, bringing real coordination and accountability to how the federal government responds to gun violence.
The movement to end gun violence is in a far stronger place today than it was eight years ago. And the gun lobby knows it. The NRA is weaker than it’s ever been — not because of any one person, but because millions of people refused to give up.
I want to be clear about something:
**I do this work to prevent what happened at Parkland from ever happening again.**
And part of how we do that is changing _who_ is in government — not just pressuring the same leaders over and over to do the right thing.
You’ve helped make that possible.
On a day that will always carry a lot of grief, I mainly want to say this: _Thank you._
Thank you for standing with me, with other survivors, and with a movement that chose persistence over hopelessness — even when it was hard.
We still have a long way to go. Progress isn’t enough, but it is real. And it saves lives.
I carry Parkland with me every day. I always will. And I’m deeply grateful that you’ve chosen to carry this fight with me. Thank you again for everything you’re doing.
David Hogg
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=1&akid=3149%2E587540%2ErNzN7v)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/update-your-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=2&akid=3149%2E587540%2ErNzN7v)

To give by check:
Leaders We Deserve
611 Pennsylvania Ave SE
#192
Washington, DC 20003
Email us: [info@leaderswedeserve.com](mailto:info@leaderswedeserve.com)
PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE.COM, NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE

body:
The latest polling shows Democrats could be competitive in traditionally Red states this November. | [](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=e00a7770c3f0453185f4492677b9f79b&_e=78VfkfIVwa7Xhql1Gz0UCBCD6_dMt_eDCuQgepi9lT81CeBLo43IZxoP-nObDMSuoSNwXgCdkN9rx8xf-z9abuRd3fvdk714jYdQV3mZNk46IafyEDeULlKf0chb2o2lxJGFJE5WlDD4UhIOY-N7bwlUch9uplCVvcI1yccLhHNqdT0R1mM8WkuOJFNdvc13UMuT73ZZUWpVgwZwUjVF0CSouEc6NWaVlnLw50K3OoxBEWxSzXXsLJdPaWJlFelV6ERreyiRjHrcxr5iMj8-SZIrSF8Pco9Wj-DDNAyZYOzGEShii6W0IcGuzTwUgi5VRiOXSpC_iBFM8fOw9m71Nh8WpPfj3CIeYVfMc2ctxCU-0Xv8GSC9vBFGn4pm6hHZpLXEbrkkab2xxEdJJCWBhw4nhzOUCfJFytUKoVcjZ3GInnLE4LG1HJxtbX__Vavj3XCwor-gpEF7as_hAON_kYI8u8ue_mFGOSDWXNfk550LavZS0M6bnotnIJi5a5Yzd0hXlpcPeyu6y1sP43cNWfOcgS40zw6ojRXY31eqrnjq-N8NwZF5P9jKwXyfw87YfXM17Pg5zy4Ex3v41sAUmgFRDqRuQ3mjxo97ReroVhGeV1UggSn1o6oBOVRK2_2NuXyjAupdHSqrh9oXvDnD6rPEKygmwEqQoO797VzWhYb9PKpar3eRkIt58y_wrEyRACzOA6W_eyuwcrVyXzYga7GdP7USugpa4fyEAgTcc5EiinzbjobWnqL2_ZcGBJP49MspZJzoNAcgeAdq2eJFbohltiWs4ULpxwd_Sp0WOWQMDraDhFyEB0UGT359O85u)

Fellow American, we cannot ignore these signs.

The 2026 Senate map is not safe - _even in Red States_ - forcing us to go on the defensive.

Chuck Schumer, Senate Democrats, and their Hollywood donors are flooding Red states with cash to flip them blue. States like Ohio, Alaska, Texas, and even Montana are in jeopardy with the money the Left is investing.

_We cannot let that happen._

In 2024, the Montana Senate race was one of the most expensive in the nation. _And Democrats outspent Republicans by tens of millions of dollars, mostly from outside dark-money groups._

This year, I am up for re-election. I made the left furious by leading Senate Republicans to flip seats in Montana, West Virginia, Ohio, and Pennsylvania.

**[They want revenge.](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=e00a7770c3f0453185f4492677b9f79b&_e=78VfkfIVwa7Xhql1Gz0UCBCD6_dMt_eDCuQgepi9lT81CeBLo43IZxoP-nObDMSuoSNwXgCdkN9rx8xf-z9abuRd3fvdk714jYdQV3mZNk46IafyEDeULlKf0chb2o2lxJGFJE5WlDD4UhIOY-N7bwlUch9uplCVvcI1yccLhHNqdT0R1mM8WkuOJFNdvc13UMuT73ZZUWpVgwZwUjVF0CSouEc6NWaVlnLw50K3OoxBEWxSzXXsLJdPaWJlFelV6ERreyiRjHrcxr5iMj8-SZIrSF8Pco9Wj-DDNAyZYOzDlUzr6xRt2Qtjy_E5i2vA8SzY_Y0wZj2DBlOq9vWumzDV9RA8KTx8LbkLyIYQDSodQcdhw5MykMWX0_tTxM9sgzc9sQJVjN_8n0z5dNU73-7IxHkrMLehaWjFkzFu5XiGCzymctV6Yxf4eevOyvs03AnDqKqln6NaDHLY_Df7PPXiC30Evr7MmzmDJ954ZlsH2MeEYftAfF_Jupu9OYajiW5UROkm3jKmBgvfE-69Lhfi70pdVZWIB_tOUTuFx8ius5LMt2F0JQIM5-R2wDhnhgUM4CiWX_sjfMIzHebeTTeLYtt7zOR0cQvSxHPZooWjoYemvvYav6PTtEkzfNVcFr7FyRmLtLFNkhyEvEX38lL-uU_ear8jEUSRQwQ-IfrclA5b9UuMDsck_Mesvo5y8Bh6-dvVftWTPdWdr8UccwdxkGVLSmbadkQD0KdNrFTlhtBtUnzlU-vg9CoYVKxOw-Oz0fjMNA9pPX-9qm-0N4XBvs2n1-9OO_qdXbzhWDQS1iEEaE71BmoOhrNVWd3t)**

**[That's why your support now – not this summer or even this fall – matters.](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=e00a7770c3f0453185f4492677b9f79b&_e=78VfkfIVwa7Xhql1Gz0UCBCD6_dMt_eDCuQgepi9lT81CeBLo43IZxoP-nObDMSuoSNwXgCdkN9rx8xf-z9abuRd3fvdk714jYdQV3mZNk46IafyEDeULlKf0chb2o2lxJGFJE5WlDD4UhIOY-N7bwlUch9uplCVvcI1yccLhHNqdT0R1mM8WkuOJFNdvc13UMuT73ZZUWpVgwZwUjVF0CSouEc6NWaVlnLw50K3OoxBEWxSzXXsLJdPaWJlFelV6ERreyiRjHrcxr5iMj8-SZIrSF8Pco9Wj-DDNAyZYOyYqewqjHMILhZxnC52VSilV7hA75kAg3nQcNCO3fVzCkIQWJTq-AuPWFckWQgtaIczSo_2XygWgBaHNeXIFWaeaZuTYl2n50a7EQrORUf993oYtKW7WedGc89eT_qoDIsa7Jctrdv0dysLGLYNWwda8kMW8WXXCkKz5pHtVZVJAyD4eNvxMXDibu5Zid0AK_F4QvKYL8LqJkQKHBsfcM3JnbN5LukeIGOEgyyZ6AAhKnfxypb7dZGTTNiC7Hpg3p98cCotOVvCRkWAW0ZNoi20-geXvo5n-GDcXw5xdLcdohA8ADEc5RDUsITx1TKhknpenan4mYtMFYkeYL3iSTPgw45jMwu8xUvzYQBcc6pemNqhXAeUwKzTyEVfuUUCu_2Ik2oVkFYbOW-yD9hQJCrUmKYNVEKbOpKtLH0-NXyBmFnrNxI1zrv1vGjh5ZCYm7Vfuf9epBGdEmWfLd8C4sKAEwWGgjqaXI6cGYDrWKfm5x7OWLY_v2cIZKAdF38XVLAIplSNAcBgc9LVfPO4kWgL) **
* We must compete with the massive sums Democrat donors are throwing into races nationwide.
* To protect President Trump's America First agenda, we cannot afford to lose in states like Montana - it's the last line of defense against Senate Democrats taking over the Senate.
* We need to be relentless - but that costs money in the form of digital ads, mail, and TV.

clean_body: The latest polling shows Democrats could be competitive in traditionally Red states this November. | Fellow American, we cannot ignore these signs. The 2026 Senate map is not safe - even in Red States - forcing us to go on the defensive. Chuck Schumer, Senate Democrats, and their Hollywood donors are flooding Red states with cash to flip them blue. States like Ohio, Alaska, Texas, and even Montana are in jeopardy with the money the Left is investing. We cannot let that happen. In 2024, the Montana Senate race was one of the most expensive in the nation. And Democrats outspent Republicans by tens of millions of dollars, mostly from outside dark-money groups. This year, I am up for re-election. I made the left furious by leading Senate Republicans to flip seats in Montana, West Virginia, Ohio, and Pennsylvania. We must compete with the massive sums Democrat donors are throwing into races nationwide. To protect President Trump's America First agenda, we cannot afford to lose in states like Montana - it's the last line of defense against Senate Democrats taking over the Senate. We need to be relentless - but that costs money in the form of digital ads, mail, and TV. The Democrats are pulling out all the stops and think this year's Senate map is good for them. Help me protect our Senate majority and fight off Schumer's blue money machine while delivering new wins for President Trump. Let's win. Steve Daines Steve Daines is a fifth-generation Montanan, life-long sportsman and father of four serving the people of Montana as their Senator. Steve is committed to always putting Montana first as he works to bring real change to Washington and fights for more jobs, less government and common sense solutions to get our country back on track. Senator Steve Daines ** | | Paid for by Steve Daines for Montana

name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: My annual heart-shaped cake
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for Senate
body:
| | | [ ](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/216?t=3&akid=2773%2E4032634%2EjSZJIO)
---
Happy Valentine’s Day. No matter how you celebrate, I hope you get the opportunity to show some love to the people you care about — and you also feel loved today. My mother was born on February 14, and she loved her special connection to Valentine’s Day. From the time they were teenage sweethearts, my daddy bought my mother a heart-shaped box of chocolates every year. In fact, I have a box of valentines that he gave her all those years ago. I loved my mother’s special connection to Valentine’s Day, too. When I was a little girl, I bought some heart-shaped pans at the dime store. It became a family tradition: Every year, I baked my mother a heart-shaped cake. Decades later, when she was in her 80s, she had some minor surgery — nothing serious. The day before she was scheduled to go home, she was in good cheer. All the kids and grandkids came to visit, and we gathered in her hospital room to tell funny stories. We laughed and had juice and cookies. Finally, Daddy sent us all home, and we left that evening expecting her to be released from the hospital the next day. Then, in the middle of the night, my brother called. He said that we lost our mother. Daddy had been sitting with her when she leaned forward and said, "Don, there's that gas pain again." I was almost too shocked to cry. I just couldn't believe it. How could this have happened? We found out that she had advanced heart disease — never diagnosed, and never treated. Despite her regular trips to the doctor for check-ups, no one had any idea. I would later learn that heart disease is the #1 killer of women. No longer considered just a “man’s disease,” doctors do a much better job screening and treating women for cardiovascular disease today than they did when my mother had her heart attack. Even though my mother is gone now, I still have my heart-shaped pans. This weekend, I baked a heart-shaped cake, and opened up the box filled with old valentines from my daddy. It’s how I remember her. |
---

And I’m also doing something more to help remember her: I’m fighting — with every bone in my body — to make sure everyone can get the health care they need to live long, healthy lives with the people they love.
It feels like the Trump administration is doing everything they can to keep that from happening, to be frank. His Big, Ugly Bill took away health care from 17 million people so he could give tax cuts for billionaires. His cuts to the NIH meant that hundreds of millions of dollars in research funding for cancer, diabetes, Alzheimer’s, and heart disease simply vanished.
So I’m staying in this fight to protect health care and get more and more patients the support they need to live long, healthy lives — no matter how hard Republican extremists try to cut it.
I’m grateful you’re in this fight by my side.
Happy Valentine’s Day,
Elizabeth

clean_body: | | | Happy Valentine’s Day. No matter how you celebrate, I hope you get the opportunity to show some love to the people you care about — and you also feel loved today. My mother was born on February 14, and she loved her special connection to Valentine’s Day. From the time they were teenage sweethearts, my daddy bought my mother a heart-shaped box of chocolates every year. In fact, I have a box of valentines that he gave her all those years ago. I loved my mother’s special connection to Valentine’s Day, too. When I was a little girl, I bought some heart-shaped pans at the dime store. It became a family tradition: Every year, I baked my mother a heart-shaped cake. Decades later, when she was in her 80s, she had some minor surgery — nothing serious. The day before she was scheduled to go home, she was in good cheer. All the kids and grandkids came to visit, and we gathered in her hospital room to tell funny stories. We laughed and had juice and cookies. Finally, Daddy sent us all home, and we left that evening expecting her to be released from the hospital the next day. Then, in the middle of the night, my brother called. He said that we lost our mother. Daddy had been sitting with her when she leaned forward and said, "Don, there's that gas pain again." I was almost too shocked to cry. I just couldn't believe it. How could this have happened? We found out that she had advanced heart disease — never diagnosed, and never treated. Despite her regular trips to the doctor for check-ups, no one had any idea. I would later learn that heart disease is the #1 killer of women. No longer considered just a “man’s disease,” doctors do a much better job screening and treating women for cardiovascular disease today than they did when my mother had her heart attack. Even though my mother is gone now, I still have my heart-shaped pans. This weekend, I baked a heart-shaped cake, and opened up the box filled with old valentines from my daddy. It’s how I remember her. | And I’m also doing something more to help remember her: I’m fighting — with every bone in my body — to make sure everyone can get the health care they need to live long, healthy lives with the people they love. It feels like the Trump administration is doing everything they can to keep that from happening, to be frank. His Big, Ugly Bill took away health care from 17 million people so he could give tax cuts for billionaires. His cuts to the NIH meant that hundreds of millions of dollars in research funding for cancer, diabetes, Alzheimer’s, and heart disease simply vanished. So I’m staying in this fight to protect health care and get more and more patients the support they need to live long, healthy lives — no matter how hard Republican extremists try to cut it. I’m grateful you’re in this fight by my side. Happy Valentine’s Day, Elizabeth | | | | | | | | | ---|---|---|--- | Paid for by Warren for Senate All content © 2026 Warren for Senate, All Rights Reserved PO Box 171375, Boston, MA 02117 | | This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com We'll miss you, but you can always .

Contributions to this account may be used to influence federal elections by making independent expenditures in support of or in opposition to federal candidates. Individuals, federal committees, corporations, and labor unions may contribute to TTPAC, Inc.’s non-contribution account without limit. Contributions by national banks, foreign nationals (without lawful permanent resident status), and other foreign entities (e.g., foreign corporations) are prohibited. Contributions to TTPAC, Inc.’s non-contribution account are not tax-deductible for federal income tax purposes.

name: Rudy Giuliani
subject: “FULL RESTITUTION”
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rudy Defense Trust
body:
A slap on the wrist is not enough...
. . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .
| **_FROM THE DESK OF RUDY W. GIULIANI_**
---
Peter,
---
This report gives me hope that justice will be served:
---
**_“Republicans are demanding _FULL_ _RESTITUTION_ for Rudy Giuliani, who was charged with 13 felony counts and fined $148 million after contesting Georgia’s 2020 election, following Fulton County’s admission that 315,000 votes were fraudulently counted.”_**
---
Since you’ve been supporting me through thick and thin, you know more than anyone that I’ve been dragged through _hell and back_ over the last 5 years.
---
**_And what was my terrible “crime?”_**
---
...As it turns out, my “crime” was speaking the __truth__ – God’s honest truth.
---
_Ballots were, in fact, WRONGLY counted. 315,000 of them to be exact – in a state decided by less than 12,000 votes!_
---
_If America becomes a country where Deep State goons and corrupt lawyers and judges can RUIN you for simply asking questions and telling the truth, then our Free Republic is _dead_._
---
**That’s why I’m not giving up the fight._And neither is President Trump_.**
---
Giving up this fight means giving up on what America stands for. It means surrendering to fear tactics and legal warfare.
---
**The bad guys who perverted our justice system cannot just get off with a slap on the wrist. Dropped charges and a dismissed prosecutor do not undo the catastrophic damage that was done.**
---
**That’s why I’m _humbly_ asking you to join Republicans who are calling for “FULL RESTITUTION” for yours truly.**
---
When these leftist crooks first had me booked in Georgia, they filed my mugshot under **booking # 2313757** ...
---
So my goal is to have **2,313,757** red-blooded Americans sign the petition for “full restitution.”
---

clean_body: A slap on the wrist is not enough... . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . | FROM THE DESK OF RUDY W. GIULIANI Peter, This report gives me hope that justice will be served: “Republicans are demanding FULL RESTITUTION for Rudy Giuliani, who was charged with 13 felony counts and fined $148 million after contesting Georgia’s 2020 election, following Fulton County’s admission that 315,000 votes were fraudulently counted.” Since you’ve been supporting me through thick and thin, you know more than anyone that I’ve been dragged through hell and back over the last 5 years. And what was my terrible “crime?” ...As it turns out, my “crime” was speaking the truth – God’s honest truth. Ballots were, in fact, WRONGLY counted. 315,000 of them to be exact – in a state decided by less than 12,000 votes! If America becomes a country where Deep State goons and corrupt lawyers and judges can RUIN you for simply asking questions and telling the truth, then our Free Republic is dead. That’s why I’m not giving up the fight.And neither is President Trump. Giving up this fight means giving up on what America stands for. It means surrendering to fear tactics and legal warfare. The bad guys who perverted our justice system cannot just get off with a slap on the wrist. Dropped charges and a dismissed prosecutor do not undo the catastrophic damage that was done. That’s why I’m humbly asking you to join Republicans who are calling for “FULL RESTITUTION” for yours truly. When these leftist crooks first had me booked in Georgia, they filed my mugshot under booking # 2313757 ... So my goal is to have 2,313,757 red-blooded Americans sign the petition for “full restitution.” Thank you and God bless you, Rudy Giuliani Rudy Defense Trust Paid for by Rudy Defense Trust

name: Rose For Congress
subject: On Love, Values, and the Work Ahead
body:
Valentine’s Day is about showing love through action.

| | | | | [|
---](https://roseforcongress.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=35bf91c695e8f4a5dc8a9dd42&id=3de4f3a7b0&e=8c53948e4c)
| | | | | |
# On Love, Values, and the Work Ahead
---
| | Happy Valentine’s Day!
Timothy and I have been married for 33 years. As I was writing that number down, I thought, wow, that’s a long time! And including the year we first met, this will be our 35th Valentine’s Day together.
That length of a marriage doesn’t happen by accident.
It comes from sharing core values and common goals, and from doing the hard work of listening, respecting differences, knowing when to give way, and standing firm when it matters.
That’s also how I believe governing should work.
**If elected to Congress, this is what I will bring with me: a commitment to our shared values - centering People, Peace, and Planet - and the persistence to work with others to turn those values into real policies. Medicare for All. Affordable housing. Strong worker rights. Ending foreign wars and ICE. A government that serves people, not corporate interests.**
The path to victory is real. But like any lasting partnership, it takes effort, trust, and showing up for one another, especially in the moments when it matters most.
If you’re able, I hope you’ll consider showing up for our campaign by volunteering, donating, or sharing our message with someone who believes in building a more caring, peaceful, and just future.
With gratitude, and wishing you a meaningful Valentine’s Day,Rose , Candidate for Congress, CA-02
PS. Valentine’s Day is about showing love through action. If you’re able to make a contribution today - whether it’s $14, $35, or whatever feels right - it’s a tangible way to stand with our People, Peace, and Planet campaign and help carry it forward. 💗

---
